title: Vashti is removed
summary_text: Ahasuerus displays the wealth of his kingdom and then holds a separate feast for the palace in Susa. When Vashti refuses to come before the king, she is deposed and a decree goes out that every man should rule his own household. The chapter sets the stage for the story by turning a private refusal into a public political shift.
key_points: The king displays his wealth and power.
Vashti refuses the king's summons.
The advisors urge a formal decree.
A search for a new queen begins.
evidence_phrases: the riches of his glorious kingdom

title: Esther becomes queen and Mordecai uncovers a plot
summary_text: Esther is chosen as queen, though she keeps her Jewish identity hidden at Mordecai's instruction. Mordecai later uncovers a plot against the king, and the matter is recorded in the royal chronicles. The chapter quietly prepares the reversal that will come later by keeping Esther and Mordecai near the throne.
key_points: Esther is selected as queen.
Mordecai keeps watch over her safety.
A plot against the king is exposed.
The act is written into the royal records.
evidence_phrases: had not made known her people
Mordecai sat in the king's gate
it was written in the book

title: Haman plots the destruction of the Jews
summary_text: Haman is elevated above the other officials, and Mordecai's refusal to bow turns his pride into murderous rage. Haman persuades the king to issue a decree that will destroy all the Jews on a chosen day. The chapter shows how a small act of refusal becomes a threat against an entire people.
key_points: Haman is promoted in the court.
Mordecai refuses to bow.
Haman seeks to destroy the Jews.
The king's seal makes the decree official.

title: Mordecai and Esther prepare to act
summary_text: Mordecai mourns in sackcloth, and Esther learns that her position in the palace cannot keep her from the crisis. He urges her to go before the king, reminding her that deliverance may arise from another place if she stays silent. The chapter turns fear into resolve through fasting and shared risk.
key_points: Mordecai grieves the decree publicly.
Esther receives Mordecai's warning.
Fasting is ordered before action.
Esther chooses to risk her life.
evidence_phrases: who knows whether you have come

title: Esther invites the king and Haman
summary_text: Esther approaches the king safely and invites him and Haman to a banquet. She delays her request, giving Haman time to be drawn deeper into his own pride, and he builds a gallows for Mordecai. The chapter uses suspense to show how quickly human arrogance can become self-destruction.
key_points: Esther is welcomed by the king.
She invites the king and Haman to a banquet.
Haman boasts of his status.
He builds a gallows for Mordecai.
evidence_phrases: Mordecai sat in the king's gate

title: The king honors Mordecai
summary_text: Unable to sleep, the king reads the chronicles and discovers that Mordecai once saved his life without reward. Haman is forced to honor the very man he hoped to destroy, and the reversal is public and humiliating. The chapter shows divine providence working through a sleepless night and a royal record book.
key_points: The king cannot sleep.
Mordecai's unrewarded service is discovered.
Haman must lead the honor parade.
The planned victim is publicly exalted.
evidence_phrases: mourning and having his head covered

title: Haman is exposed and hanged
summary_text: At the second banquet Esther reveals Haman's plot and identifies herself as part of the people marked for death. The king rises in wrath, and Haman is executed on the gallows he prepared for Mordecai. The chapter delivers a sharp reversal of power and a public exposure of evil.
key_points: Esther identifies herself with the doomed people.
She exposes Haman's scheme.
The king leaves in anger.
Haman dies on his own gallows.
evidence_phrases: let my life be given me
the king arose in his wrath

title: A counter-decree gives the Jews defense
summary_text: Esther and Mordecai receive the house of Haman, but the deeper problem is the irreversible decree against the Jews. A new royal edict authorizes the Jews to defend themselves, giving the people legal room to survive. The chapter turns grief into organized protection through letter-writing and royal authority.
key_points: Mordecai receives Haman's estate.
Esther pleads for her people again.
A new decree authorizes defense.
Mordecai is raised in royal honor.

title: Purim is established after victory
summary_text: The Jews defeat their enemies throughout the provinces, and the day of planned destruction becomes a day of triumph. Purim is established as a recurring remembrance of deliverance, with the lot that threatened them now marking their rescue. The chapter gives the feast its meaning through public memory and celebration.
key_points: The Jews defend themselves successfully.
Purim is established as an annual feast.
The memory of deliverance is formalized.
The victory is recorded for future generations.
evidence_phrases: the day that the enemies hoped

title: Mordecai's greatness
summary_text: The book closes with Mordecai's rising honor and wide influence in the Persian realm. He works for the good of his people and speaks peace to their descendants. The final chapter leaves the story with a stable ending rather than a final battle, showing that deliverance can lead into lasting service.
key_points: Mordecai is greatly honored.
His influence reaches the empire.
He seeks the welfare of his people.
The book ends with peace for his descendants.
evidence_phrases: seeking the wealth of his people
